title: Add error handling to SearchAsync and SearchMemoryTool
description:
## Problem
`RestMemoryGateway.SearchAsync` (line 36-48) has zero error handling:
```csharp
var hits = await http.GetFromJsonAsync<SearchHit[]>(url, JsonOpts, cancellationToken) ?? [];
```
No try/catch for HttpRequestException, TaskCanceledException, or JsonException. `SearchMemoryTool.ExecuteAsync` (line 54) also has no try/catch. A transient network blip crashes the tool execution loop.

## Desired Outcome
Search failures return empty results (logged as warnings) rather than throwing.

## Scope
- `RestMemoryGateway.SearchAsync` — add try/catch, log warning, return empty on recoverable errors
- `SearchMemoryTool.ExecuteAsync` — wrap in try/catch, return `ToolResult(false, ...)` on failure
- Exception: propagate `OperationCanceledException` (cancellation is intentional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTTP 500 from MemorySmith returns empty results, not throw
- [ ] Network timeout returns empty results, not throw
- [ ] SearchMemoryTool returns `ToolResult(false, "Search failed: {message}")` on error
- [ ] Unit test verifies graceful degradation
- [ ] All existing tests pass
